summary: Cart trade-pricing & VAT-label regression — all 7 frontend scenarios PASSED on the live preview at /shop/tile-cart. The new canonical retail_price_inc_vat + cartPricing.js helpers correctly re-derive the displayed price live whenever trade auth flips, with no double-VAT bugs and consistent VAT labels in both states.
critical_code_review_comments: TileCartContext.loadCartFromStorage filters items by hasValidPrice (price > 0). Trade carts persisted with retail_price_inc_vat are fine because price is also stored, but if a future migration ever drops `price` while keeping retail_price_inc_vat, the validator will silently wipe the cart. Consider relaxing the filter to accept `(price > 0 || retail_price_inc_vat > 0)`.

cartPricing.getRetailIncVat treats item.isTrade strictly truthy — if a future code path stores `isTrade: 'true'` (string) it'll still work, but if it stores `is_trade` (snake_case) the helper will mis-infer and treat the trade ex-VAT figure as retail inc-VAT, under-charging. Worth a defensive `item.isTrade || item.is_trade` if the snake_case naming bleeds in.

TileCartPage.totalIncVat uses `Math.round((totalExVat + vatAmount) * 100) / 100`. Since vatAmount is itself already rounded, this can drift by 1p vs `totalExVat * 1.20` for some rounding edge cases. Verified in tests: 159.16 + 31.83 = 190.99 (ex × 1.20 = 190.992 → 190.99) — fine here, but worth a comment that the displayed inc-VAT figure is the sum of the two displayed-rounded rows by design (matches receipt).

creditBackRates POST /api/shop/cart/credit-back-rates returns 400 for unknown slugs (visible in console during seeded-cart tests). Not a regression and the UI silently falls back to defaultCreditBackRate, but the endpoint should probably 200 with an empty rates map for unknown slugs rather than 400.

The 'Saved vs retail' percentage (TileCartPage line 778) divides by (subtotal + savedVsRetail). For a single-line single-discount cart this is fine, but the math becomes misleading when delivery is included or when items have mixed trade_discount values — consider clarifying the copy as '% off product retail (ex. delivery)'.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7: pre-login retail view, trade re-pricing on login, VAT-label consistency, trade logout reverts, click-and-collect zeros delivery, dynamic delivery fee £79.99, legacy isTrade item infer + no double-discount, page reload preserves state, multi-item per-line trade_discount math, saved-vs-retail accuracy)

test_credentials: Trade customer simulated via localStorage shortcut: tile_shop_token='t', tile_shop_customer={email,is_trade:true,trade_discount:5,business_name}. admin@test.com / admin123 not exercised this round (no admin-side checks needed — checkout-settings already returns default_fee=79.99).
seed_data_creation: Only localStorage seeds (tilestation_cart) — nothing persisted server-side.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable Playwright recipe at /shop/tile-cart: (1) seed `tilestation_cart` in localStorage with [{id,slug,display_name,price,retail_price_inc_vat,priceType:'room_lot',quantity,sqm_per_box,tiles_per_box,pricing_unit,is_surface_product}], (2) goto /shop/tile-cart, (3) flip trade auth via `localStorage.setItem('tile_shop_token','t'); localStorage.setItem('tile_shop_customer',JSON.stringify({...,is_trade:true,trade_discount:N})); window.dispatchEvent(new Event('trade-auth-change'));` — no reload needed, useTradeUser hook re-renders live. Key data-testids: cart-subtotal, total-regular (logged-out), total-ex-vat, vat-amount, total-inc-vat, saved-vs-retail-amount, delivery-option, collection-option, free-delivery-progress-wrap, free-sample-nudge, cart-save-banner. Legacy fallback path covered by item.isTrade=true + missing retail_price_inc_vat — getRetailIncVat in /app/frontend/src/utils/cartPricing.js correctly back-computes inc-VAT retail from trade-ex-VAT price.
rca of the issue: Original bugs RCA: cart only persisted item.price as a single field, so when a guest added a £100 retail-inc-VAT item and later signed in as trade, the cart kept showing 100.00 but Order Summary started labeling it 'ex. VAT' and added 20% on top → £120 effective — overcharging. Fix: persist a canonical retail_price_inc_vat at addToCart time and DERIVE the displayed price live via getEffectivePrice(item, isTrade, td). Verified end-to-end: log in/out toggles re-render the cart with correct math in both directions, including legacy items where retail_price_inc_vat is missing (inferred from item.price + item.isTrade + trade_discount). Bonus fix verified: delivery row now reads £{defaultDeliveryFee} (admin-config £79.99) instead of hardcoded £49.99.
